title: Ensure that the admission control policy is set to SecurityContextDeny

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control policy is set to SecurityContextDeny
input_row.description: Restrict pod level SecurityContext customization. Instead of using a customized SecurityContext for your pods, use a Pod Security Policy (PSP), which is a cluster-level resource that controls the actions that a pod can perform and what it has the ability to access.
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to SecurityContextDeny denies the pod level SecurityContext customization. Any attempts to customize the SecurityContexts that are not explicitly defined in the Pod Security Policy (PSP) are blocked. This ensures that all the pods adhere to the PSP defined by your organization and you have a uniform pod level security posture.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --admission-control argument is set to a value that includes SecurityContextDeny .
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/apiserver file on the master node and set the KUBE_ADMISSION_CONTROL parameter to "--admission- control=...,SecurityContextDeny,..." : KUBE_ADMISSION_CONTROL="--admission-control=...,SecurityContextDeny,..." Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Impact: None 39 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions verifying the `--admission-control` argument for the kube-apiserver, which can be checked via Kubernetes API or kubectl commands.
- The requirement to check for the presence of `SecurityContextDeny` in the admission control policy is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The control can be verified without subjective review or host access.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_control_security_context_deny_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following command to check the kube-apiserver configuration:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- The specific property to validate is that the output includes `--admission-control=...,SecurityContextDeny,...`.
- **Pass Criteria:** The output must contain `SecurityContextDeny` as part of the admission control arguments.
- **Fail Criteria:** The output does not contain `SecurityContextDeny`.
